member.name: T:ContractFunctionResult
member.summary:
Result of invoking a contract via {@link ContractCallQuery}, or {@link ContractExecuteTransaction}, or the result of a contract constructor being called by {@link ContractCreateTransaction}.

If you require a type which is not supported here, please let us know on this Github issue.

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.#ctor(Proto.ContractFunctionResult)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.ContractId
member.summary: The ID of the contract that was invoked.

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.EvmAddress
member.summary: The contract's 20-byte EVM address

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.ErrorMessage
member.summary: message in case there was an error during smart contract execution

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.Bloom
member.summary: bloom filter for record

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.GasUsed
member.summary: units of gas used to execute contract

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.logs
member.summary: the log info for events returned by the function

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.CreatedContractIds
member.summary: The created ids will now _also_ be externalized through internal transaction records, where each record has its alias field populated with the new contract's EVM address. (This is needed for contracts created with CREATE2, since there is no longer a simple relationship between the new contract's 0.0.X id and its Solidity address.)

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.StateChanges
member.remarks: @deprecated- Use mirror node for contract traceability instead

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.Gas
member.summary: The amount of gas available for the call, aka the gasLimit

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.HbarAmount
member.summary: Number of tinybars sent (the function must be payable if this is nonzero).

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.ContractFunctionParametersBytes
member.summary:
The parameters passed into the contract call.
This field should only be populated when the paired TransactionBody in the record stream is not a ContractCreateTransactionBody or a ContractCallTransactionBody.

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.SenderAccountId
member.summary: The account that is the "sender." If not present it is the accountId from the transactionId.

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.ContractNonces
member.summary: A list of updated contract account nonces containing the new nonce value for each contract account. This is always empty in a ContractCallLocalResponse#ContractFunctionResult message, since no internal creations can happen in a static EVM call.

member.name: F:ContractFunctionResult.SignerNonce
member.summary: If not null this field specifies what the value of the signer account nonce is post transaction execution. For transactions that don't update the signer nonce (like HAPI ContractCall and ContractCreate transactions) this field should be null.

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.AsBytes
member.summary: Get the whole raw function result.

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetBytes(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the nth value in the result as a dynamic byte array.
member.param: The index of the array of bytes to be retrieved

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetBytes32(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth fixed-width 32-byte value in the result.

This is the native word size for the Solidity ABI.

The index of the array of bytes to be retrieved byte[]

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetBool(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the nth value as a bool.
member.param: The index of the bool to be retrieved

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetInt8(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as an 8-bit integer.

If the actual value is wider it will be truncated to the last byte (similar to Java's integer narrowing semantics).

If you are developing a contract and intending to return more than one of these values from a Solidity function, consider using the {@code bytes32} Solidity type instead as that will be a more compact representation which will save on gas. (Each individual {@code int8} value is padded to 32 bytes in the ABI.)

The index of the value to be retrieved byte

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetInt32(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 32-bit integer.

If the actual value is wider it will be truncated to the last 4 bytes (similar to Java's integer narrowing semantics).

The index of the value to be retrieved int

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetInt64(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 64-bit integer.

If the actual value is wider it will be truncated to the last 8 bytes (similar to Java's integer narrowing semantics).

The index of the value to be retrieved long

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetInt256(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 256-bit integer.

This type can represent the full width of Solidity integers.

The index of the value to be retrieved BigInteger

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetUint8(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 8-bit unsigned integer.

If the actual value is wider it will be truncated to the last byte (similar to Java's integer narrowing semantics).

Because Java does not have native unsigned integers, this is semantically identical to {@link #getInt8(int)}. To treat the value as unsigned in the range {@code [0, 255]}, use {@link Byte#toUnsignedInt(byte)} to widen to {@code int} without sign-extension.

If you are developing a contract and intending to return more than one of these values from a Solidity function, consider using the {@code bytes32} Solidity type instead as that will be a more compact representation which will save on gas. (Each individual {@code uint8} value is padded to 32 bytes in the ABI.)

The index of the value to be retrieved byte

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetUint32(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 32-bit unsigned integer.

If the actual value is wider it will be truncated to the last 4 bytes (similar to Java's integer narrowing semantics).

Because Java does not have native unsigned integers, this is semantically identical to {@link #getInt32(int)}. The {@link Integer} class has static methods for treating an {@code int} as unsigned where the difference between signed and unsigned actually matters (comparison, division, printing and widening to {@code long}).

The index of the value to be retrieved int

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetUint64(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 64-bit integer.

If the actual value is wider it will be truncated to the last 8 bytes (similar to Java's integer narrowing semantics).

Because Java does not have native unsigned integers, this is semantically identical to {@link #getInt64(int)}. The {@link Long} class has static methods for treating a {@code long} as unsigned where the difference between signed and unsigned actually matters (comparison, division and printing).

The index of the value to be retrieved long

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetUint256(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Get the nth returned value as a 256-bit unsigned integer.

The value will be padded with a leading zero-byte so that {@link BigInteger#BigInteger(byte[])} treats the value as positive regardless of whether the most significant bit is set or not.

This type can represent the full width of Solidity integers.

The index of the value to be retrieved BigInteger

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetAddress(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the nth returned value as a Solidity address.
member.param: The index of the value to be retrieved

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetString(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the nth returned value as a string
member.param: The index of the string to be retrieved

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.GetStringArray(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the nth returned value as a list of strings
member.param: The index of the list of strings to be retrieved

member.name: M:ContractFunctionResult.ToProtobuf
member.summary: Create the protobuf representation.
